payload.summary: Planned enforcement of the local CLI mutation boundary so shared checkout files remain read-only outside explicit sync-point operations. The selected approach adds a reusable checkout policy helper, wires mutating skills to worktree guards, and updates operator documentation plus invariant tests.

decisions.title: Use a shared checkout policy helper
decisions.rationale: A single helper makes the worktree invariant executable and reusable across Python scripts and SKILL.md shell steps.

decisions.title: Keep sync-point exceptions explicit
decisions.rationale: Merge and post-merge operations still need shared-checkout access, but only with clean-tree and active-agent guards.

next_steps: Implement skills/shared/checkout_policy.py and its tests first.
Update mutating skill instructions and autopilot phase isolation metadata.
Sync canonical skills to runtime copies after canonical SKILL.md edits.

relevant_files.description: design decisions for the policy guard
